{\n193\t\t\t\th.PutObjectRetention(w, r, bucket, key)\n194\t\t\t\treturn\n195\t\t\t}\n196\t\t\t// Handle PutObjectLegalHold (PUT ?legal-hold on object)\n197\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"legal-hold\") && key != \"\" {\n198\t\t\t\th.PutObjectLegalHold(w, r, bucket, key)\n199\t\t\t\treturn\n200\t\t\t}\n201\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n202\t\t\t\t// Handle UploadPart (PUT ?partNumber&uploadId on object) — standard\n203\t\t\t\t// S3 clients PUT parts; routing this to PutObject silently stored\n204\t\t\t\t// each part as the whole object and left the B2 multipart upload\n205\t\t\t\t// empty, so CompleteMultipartUpload always failed with InvalidPart.\n206\t\t\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n207\t\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n208\t\t\t\t\treturn\n209\t\t\t\t}\n210\t\t\t\t// Check for CopyObject (has x-amz-copy-source header)\n211\t\t\t\tif r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\") != \"\" {\n212\t\t\t\t\th.CopyObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n213\t\t\t\t} else {\n214\t\t\t\t\th.PutObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n215\t\t\t\t}\n216\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n217\t\t\t\th.CreateBucket(w, r, bucket)\n218\t\t\t}\n219\t\tcase http.MethodHead:\n220\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n221\t\t\t\th.HeadObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n222\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n223\t\t\t\th.HeadBucket(w, r, bucket)\n224\t\t\t}\n225\t\tcase http.MethodDelete:\n226\t\t\t// Handle DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration (DELETE ?lifecycle on bucket)\n227\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"lifecycle\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n228\t\t\t\th.DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n229\t\t\t\treturn\n230\t\t\t}\n231\t\t\t// Handle AbortMultipartUpload (DELETE ?uploadId on object)\n232\t\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && key != \"\" {\n233\t\t\t\th.AbortMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n234\t\t\t\treturn\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n237\t\t\t\th.DeleteObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n238\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n239\t\t\t\th.DeleteBucket(w, r, bucket)\n240\t\t\t}\n241\t\tcase http.MethodPost:\n242\t\t\t// Handle multipart upload operations\n243\t\t\tuploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\")\n244\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"uploads\") {\n245\t\t\t\th.CreateMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key)\n246\t\t\t} else if uploadID != \"\" {\n247\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n248\t\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n249\t\t\t\t} else {\n250\t\t\t\t\th.CompleteMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n251\t\t\t\t}\n252\t\t\t} else if r.URL.Query().Has(\"delete\") {\n253\t\t\t\t// DeleteObjects (bulk delete) - uses POST with ?delete query param\n254\t\t\t\th.DeleteObjects(w, r, bucket)\n255\t\t\t} else {\n256\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Unsupported POST operation\", 400)\n257\t\t\t}\n258\t\tdefault:\n259\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MethodNotAllowed\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Method %s not allowed\", r.Method), 405)\n260\t\t}\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// PutObject handles S3 PutObject with encryption.\n264\t// For small files (<10MB), it buffers in memory.\n265\t// For larger files, it uses streaming encryption via temp files to avoid\n266\t// loading the entire file into memory.\n267\tfunc (h *Handlers) PutObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n268\t\tctx := r.Context()\n269\t\n270\t\t// Check Content-Length header\n271\t\tcontentLength := r.ContentLength\n272\t\tstreamingThreshold := int64(10 * 1024 * 1024) // 10MB threshold\n273\t\n274\t\t// Use streaming for large files or unknown size\n275\t\tuseStreaming := contentLength < 0 || contentLength > streamingThreshold\n276\t\n277\t\tif useStreaming {\n278\t\t\th.putObjectStreaming(ctx, w, r, bucket, key)\n279\t\t\treturn\n280\t\t}\n281\t\n282\t\t// Small file: buffer in memory\n283\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n284\t\tif err != nil {\n285\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n286\t\t\treturn\n287\t\t}\n288\t\n289\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n290\t\n291\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n292\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n293\t\tif err != nil {\n294\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n295\t\t\treturn\n296\t\t}\n297\t\n298\t\t// Generate DEK and IV\n299\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n300\t\tif err != nil {\n301\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n302\t\t\treturn\n303\t\t}\n304\t\n305\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n306\t\tif err != nil {\n307\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n308\t\t\treturn\n309\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n312\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n313\t\tif err != nil {\n314\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n315\t\t\treturn\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n319\t\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(plaintext)\n320\t\n321\t\t// Create envelope header\n322\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n323\t\tif err != nil {\n324\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n325\t\t\treturn\n326\t\t}\n327\t\n328\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n329\t\tif err != nil {\n330\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n331\t\t\treturn\n332\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\t// Encrypt data\n335\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n336\t\tif err != nil {\n337\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n338\t\t\treturn\n339\t\t}\n340\t\n341\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n342\t\tif err != nil {\n343\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n344\t\t\treturn\n345\t\t}\n346\t\n347\t\t// Build envelope: header + encrypted blocks + HMAC table\n348\t\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + int64(len(encrypted)) + int64(len(hmacTable))\n349\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, envelopeSize)\n350\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n351\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n352\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n353\t\n354\t\t// Compute plaintext ETag (MD5)\n355\t\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(plaintext)\n356\t\n357\t\t// Build metadata\n358\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n359\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n360\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n361\t\t}\n362\t\n363\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n364\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n365\t\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n366\t\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n367\t\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n368\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n369\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n370\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n371\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n372\t\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n373\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n374\t\n375\t\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied\n376\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n377\t\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n378\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n379\t\t\treturn\n380\t\t}\n381\t\n382\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n383\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n384\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n385\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t// Record provenance\n388\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n389\t\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n390\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put\")\n391\t\t}\n392\t\n393\t\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n394\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n395\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n396\t\t}\n397\t\n398\t\t// Return ETag\n399\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n400\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t// putObjectStreaming handles large file uploads with streaming encryption.\n404\t// It uses a temp file to avoid loading the entire plaintext into memory.\n405\t// The process is:\n406\t// 1. Stream request body to temp file while computing SHA-256\n407\t// 2. Create envelope header with the computed SHA-256\n408\t// 3. Stream from temp file through encryption to B2 via io.Pipe\n409\t// 4. Clean up temp file\n410\tfunc (h *Handlers) putObjectStreaming(ctx context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n411\t\t// Phase 1: Stream to temp file and compute SHA-256\n412\t\ttmpFile, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-upload-*.tmp\")\n413\t\tif err != nil {\n414\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n415\t\t\treturn\n416\t\t}\n417\t\ttmpPath := tmpFile.Name()\n418\t\tdefer os.Remove(tmpPath) // Clean up on exit\n419\t\n420\t\t// Compute SHA-256 while copying to temp file\n421\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n422\t\tteeReader := io.TeeReader(r.Body, plaintextHash)\n423\t\n424\t\tplaintextSize, err := io.Copy(tmpFile, teeReader)\n425\t\tif err != nil {\n426\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n427\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n428\t\t\treturn\n429\t\t}\n430\t\n431\t\t// Get the computed SHA-256\n432\t\tvar plaintextSHA [32]byte\n433\t\tcopy(plaintextSHA[:], plaintextHash.Sum(nil))\n434\t\n435\t\t// Seek back to beginning of temp file for reading\n436\t\tif _, err := tmpFile.Seek(0, 0); err != nil {\n437\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n438\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to seek temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n439\t\t\treturn\n440\t\t}\n441\t\n442\t\t// Phase 2: Get encryption keys\n443\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n444\t\tif err != nil {\n445\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n446\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n447\t\t\treturn\n448\t\t}\n449\t\n450\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n451\t\tif err != nil {\n452\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n453\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n454\t\t\treturn\n455\t\t}\n456\t\n457\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n458\t\tif err != nil {\n459\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n460\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n461\t\t\treturn\n462\t\t}\n463\t\n464\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n465\t\tif err != nil {\n466\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n467\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n468\t\t\treturn\n469\t\t}\n470\t\n471\t\t// Create envelope header\n472\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n473\t\tif err != nil {\n474\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n475\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n476\t\t\treturn\n477\t\t}\n478\t\n479\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n480\t\tif err != nil {\n481\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n482\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n483\t\t\treturn\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\t// Create encryptor\n487\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n488\t\tif err != nil {\n489\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n490\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n491\t\t\treturn\n492\t\t}\n493\t\n494\t\t// Calculate envelope size\n495\t\tblockCount := crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize)\n496\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n497\t\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + plaintextSize + hmacTableSize\n498\t\n499\t\t// Phase 3: Stream encrypt via io.Pipe\n500\t\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n501\t\n502\t\t// Start encryption goroutine\n503\t\tencErr := make(chan error, 1)\n504\t\tgo func() {\n505\t\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n506\t\t\tdefer close(encErr)\n507\t\n508\t\t\t// Write header\n509\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(headerBytes); err != nil {\n510\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write header: %w\", err)\n511\t\t\t\treturn\n512\t\t\t}\n513\t\n514\t\t\t// Stream encrypt the plaintext\n515\t\t\thmacTable, err := encryptor.EncryptStream(tmpFile, pw, plaintextSize)\n516\t\t\tif err != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"encryption failed: %w\", err)\n518\t\t\t\treturn\n519\t\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t\t// Write HMAC table\n522\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(hmacTable); err != nil {\n523\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write HMAC table: %w\", err)\n524\t\t\t\treturn\n525\t\t\t}\n526\t\n527\t\t\tencErr <- nil\n528\t\t}()\n529\t\n530\t\t// Build metadata\n531\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n532\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n533\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n534\t\t}\n535\t\n536\t\t// Compute ETag while we have the temp file\n537\t\t// We need to read the file again for MD5, but we already have SHA-256\n538\t\t// Use SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes as ETag for streaming (non-standard but works)\n539\t\tetag := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:16])\n540\t\n541\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n542\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n543\t\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n544\t\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n545\t\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n546\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n547\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n548\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n549\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n550\t\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n551\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n552\t\n553\t\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied using streaming reader\n554\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n555\t\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, pr, envelopeSize, meta); err != nil {\n556\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n557\t\t\t// Check if there was an encryption error\n558\t\t\tselect {\n559\t\t\tcase encErrVal := <-encErr:\n560\t\t\t\tif encErrVal != nil {\n561\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n562\t\t\t\t\treturn\n563\t\t\t\t}\n564\t\t\tdefault:\n565\t\t\t}\n566\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n567\t\t\treturn\n568\t\t}\n569\t\n570\t\t// Close temp file\n571\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n572\t\n573\t\t// Check for encryption errors\n574\t\tif encErrVal := <-encErr; encErrVal != nil {\n575\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n576\t\t\treturn\n577\t\t}\n578\t\n579\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n580\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n581\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n582\t\t}\n583\t\n584\t\t// Record provenance\n585\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n586\t\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n587\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put-streaming\")\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n591\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n592\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n593\t\t}\n594\t\n595\t\t// Return ETag\n596\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n597\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Streaming\", \"true\")\n598\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n599\t}\n600\t\n601\t// GetObject handles S3 GetObject with decryption and range support.\n602\tfunc (h *Handlers) GetObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n603\t\tctx := r.Context()\n604\t\n605\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n606\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n607\t\n608\t\t// Get metadata first\n609\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n610\t\tif err != nil {\n611\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n612\t\t\treturn\n613\t\t}\n614\t\n615\t\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n616\t\t\t// Check conditional request headers for non-ARMOR objects\n617\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, info.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n618\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n619\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n620\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n621\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n622\t\t\t\t} else {\n623\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n624\t\t\t\t}\n625\t\t\t\treturn\n626\t\t\t}\n627\t\n628\t\t\t// Passthrough for non-ARMOR objects\n629\t\t\tbody, _, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n630\t\t\tif err != nil {\n631\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), 500)\n632\t\t\t\treturn\n633\t\t\t}\n634\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n635\t\n636\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(info.Size, 10))\n637\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n638\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n639\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n640\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n641\t\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n642\t\t\treturn\n643\t\t}\n644\t\n645\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n646\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n647\t\tif !ok {\n648\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n649\t\t\treturn\n650\t\t}\n651\t\n652\t\t// Get the MEK for this object using the key ID from metadata\n653\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n654\t\tif err != nil {\n655\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n656\t\t\treturn\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n660\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n661\t\tif err != nil {\n662\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n663\t\t\treturn\n664\t\t}\n665\t\n666\t\t// Create decryptor\n667\t\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n668\t\tif err != nil {\n669\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create decryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n670\t\t\treturn\n671\t\t}\n672\t\n673\t\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n674\t\n675\t\t// Check conditional request headers\n676\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, armorMeta.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n677\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n678\t\t\t\t// 304 Not Modified - set headers but no body\n679\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n680\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n681\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n682\t\t\t} else {\n683\t\t\t\t// 412 Precondition Failed\n684\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n685\t\t\t}\n686\t\t\treturn\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Check for range request\n690\t\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n691\t\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n692\t\t\th.handleRangeRequest(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified)\n693\t\t\treturn\n694\t\t}\n695\t\n696\t\t// Full object download with pipelined stream decryption\n697\t\th.handleFullObjectStream(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified)\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\t// handleFullObjectStream handles full object downloads with pipelined stream decryption.\n701\t// This uses io.Pipe to decrypt blocks as they stream from Cloudflare, reducing\n702\t// time-to-first-byte and memory usage compared to buffering the entire envelope.\n703\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleFullObjectStream(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time) {\n704\t\tctx := r.Context()\n705\t\n706\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n707\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n708\t\n709\t\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n710\t\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\n711\t\n712\t\t// Calculate offsets\n713\t\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n714\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n715\t\tdataSize := plaintextSize\n716\t\n717\t\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table (small range read)\n718\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n719\t\tif err != nil {\n720\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n721\t\t\treturn\n722\t\t}\n723\t\thmacTable, err := io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n724\t\thmacBody.Close()\n725\t\tif err != nil {\n726\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n727\t\t\treturn\n728\t\t}\n729\t\n730\t\t// 2. Start streaming data from Cloudflare (header + encrypted blocks, stop before HMAC)\n731\t\tstreamSize := crypto.HeaderSize + dataSize\n732\t\tdataBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\n733\t\tif err != nil {\n734\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), 500)\n735\t\t\treturn\n736\t\t}\n737\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n738\t\n739\t\t// 3. Read and discard the 64-byte header\n740\t\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n741\t\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n742\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), 500)\n743\t\t\treturn\n744\t\t}\n745\t\n746\t\t// Parse header to get plaintext SHA for verification\n747\t\theader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(headerBuf)\n748\t\tif err != nil {\n749\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode header: %v\", err), 500)\n750\t\t\treturn\n751\t\t}\n752\t\n753\t\t// 4. Set response headers before streaming\n754\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(plaintextSize, 10))\n755\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n756\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n757\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n758\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n759\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Stream\", \"pipelined\")\n760\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n761\t\n762\t\t// 5. Stream decrypt using io.Pipe\n763\t\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n764\t\n765\t\t// Start decryption goroutine\n766\t\tgo func() {\n767\t\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n768\t\n769\t\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n770\t\t\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n771\t\n772\t\t\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n773\t\t\t\t// Calculate actual block size (last block may be smaller)\n774\t\t\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n775\t\t\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n776\t\n777\t\t\t\t// Read encrypted block\n778\t\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n779\t\t\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n780\t\t\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n781\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"read error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n782\t\t\t\t\treturn\n783\t\t\t\t}\n784\t\t\t\tif n == 0 {\n785\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n786\t\t\t\t}\n787\t\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n788\t\n789\t\t\t\t// Verify HMAC\n790\t\t\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n791\t\t\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n792\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC table too short at block %d\", blockIndex))\n793\t\t\t\t\treturn\n794\t\t\t\t}\n795\t\t\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n796\t\n797\t\t\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n798\t\t\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n799\t\t\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n800\t\t\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n801\t\t\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n802\t\t\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n803\t\n804\t\t\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n805\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"block %d: HMAC verification failed\", blockIndex))\n806\t\t\t\t\treturn\n807\t\t\t\t}\n808\t\n809\t\t\t\t// Decrypt block (need to use CTR stream)\n810\t\t\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n811\t\t\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n812\t\t\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n813\t\t\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n814\t\n815\t\t\t\t// Update plaintext hash for verification\n816\t\t\t\tplaintextHash.Write(decrypted)\n817\t\n818\t\t\t\t// Write plaintext to pipe\n819\t\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(decrypted); err != nil {\n820\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"write error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n821\t\t\t\t\treturn\n822\t\t\t\t}\n823\t\t\t}\n824\t\n825\t\t\t// Verify plaintext SHA-256\n826\t\t\tcomputedSHA := plaintextHash.Sum(nil)\n827\t\t\tif !bytes.Equal(computedSHA, header.PlaintextSHA[:]) {\n828\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA-256 mismatch\"))\n829\t\t\t\treturn\n830\t\t\t}\n831\t\t}()\n832\t\n833\t\t// Stream plaintext to client\n834\t\tio.Copy(w, pr)\n835\t}\n836\t\n837\t// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\n838\tfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n839\t\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n840\t\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n841\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n842\t\treturn counter\n843\t}\n844\t\n845\tfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n846\t\tif a < b {\n847\t\t\treturn a\n848\t\t}\n849\t\treturn b\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t// handleRangeRequest handles range read requests.\n853\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time) {\n854\t\tctx := r.Context()\n855\t\n856\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n857\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n858\t\n859\t\t// Parse range header (bytes=start-end)\n860\t\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n861\t\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n862\t\tif err != nil {\n863\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRange\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid range: %v\", err), 400)\n864\t\t\treturn\n865\t\t}\n866\t\n867\t\t// Check if this is a Parquet footer request and we have it cached\n868\t\t// DuckDB reads footer in two steps: last 8 bytes, then footer body\n869\t\t// Footer is at the end of the file: [footer_metadata][footer_length (4B)][PAR1 (4B)]\n870\t\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 {\n871\t\t\t// This range includes the end of the file - could be a footer read\n872\t\t\tif footer, ok := h.footerCache.Get(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag); ok {\n873\t\t\t\t// We have a cached footer, serve from cache\n874\t\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(footer))\n875\t\t\t\tif start >= footerStart {\n876\t\t\t\t\t// Request is entirely within the cached footer\n877\t\t\t\t\toffset := start - footerStart\n878\t\t\t\t\tfooterEnd := offset + (end - start + 1)\n879\t\t\t\t\tif footerEnd <= int64(len(footer)) {\n880\t\t\t\t\t\tplaintext := footer[offset:footerEnd]\n881\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n882\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n883\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n884\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n885\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n886\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Footer-Cache\", \"HIT\")\n887\t\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n888\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n889\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n890\t\t\t\t\t}\n891\t\t\t\t}\n892\t\t\t}\n893\t\t}\n894\t\n895\t\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n896\t\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n897\t\tif err != nil {\n898\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), 500)\n899\t\t\treturn\n900\t\t}\n901\t\n902\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel using errgroup.\n903\t\t// This cuts range-read latency nearly in half for cache misses.\n904\t\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n905\t\n906\t\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n907\t\n908\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n909\t\t\tencryptedBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n910\t\t\tif err != nil {\n911\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n912\t\t\t}\n913\t\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n914\t\n915\t\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n916\t\t\tif err != nil {\n917\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n918\t\t\t}\n919\t\t\treturn nil\n920\t\t})\n921\t\n922\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n923\t\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n924\t\t\tif err != nil {\n925\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n926\t\t\t}\n927\t\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n928\t\n929\t\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n930\t\t\tif err != nil {\n931\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n932\t\t\t}\n933\t\t\treturn nil\n934\t\t})\n935\t\n936\t\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n937\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", err.Error(), 500)\n938\t\t\treturn\n939\t\t}\n940\t\n941\t\t// Decrypt range\n942\t\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize)\n943\t\tif err != nil {\n944\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), 500)\n945\t\t\treturn\n946\t\t}\n947\t\n948\t\t// Cache Parquet footer if this looks like a footer read\n949\t\t// Footer is detected by: 1) Reading near end of file, 2) Data ends with \"PAR1\" magic\n950\t\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 && len(plaintext) >= 8 {\n951\t\t\tif backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n952\t\t\t\t// This is a Parquet file, try to cache the full footer\n953\t\t\t\t// If we just read the last 8 bytes, cache it for footer length detection\n954\t\t\t\t// If we read more, it might be the full footer\n955\t\t\t\th.cacheParquetFooter(ctx, bucket, key, armorMeta, plaintext, plaintextSize)\n956\t\t\t}\n957\t\t}\n958\t\n959\t\t// Set response headers\n960\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n961\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n962\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n963\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n964\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n965\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n966\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n967\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n968\t}\n969\t\n970\t// cacheParquetFooter caches Parquet footer data for faster subsequent reads.\n971\tfunc (h *Handlers) cacheParquetFooter(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintext []byte, plaintextSize int64) {\n972\t\t// If we got the last 8 bytes, we can determine footer length\n973\t\tif len(plaintext) == 8 {\n974\t\t\t// This is the footer length read - cache a small marker\n975\t\t\t// The actual footer will be cached on the next read\n976\t\t\treturn\n977\t\t}\n978\t\n979\t\t// Check if we have the full footer by verifying PAR1 magic at end\n980\t\tif len(plaintext) >= 8 && backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n981\t\t\t// We have at least part of the footer\n982\t\t\t// Parse footer length from the last 8 bytes if available\n983\t\t\tfooterRange, err := backend.GetParquetFooterRange(plaintext[len(plaintext)-8:], plaintextSize)\n984\t\t\tif err != nil {\n985\t\t\t\treturn\n986\t\t\t}\n987\t\n988\t\t\t// Check if we have the complete footer\n989\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(footerRange.Length) - 8\n990\t\t\trequestStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(plaintext))\n991\t\n992\t\t\tif requestStart <= footerStart {\n993\t\t\t\t// We have the complete footer, extract and cache it\n994\t\t\t\tfooterOffset := footerStart - requestStart\n995\t\t\t\tif footerOffset >= 0 && int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8 <= len(plaintext) {\n996\t\t\t\t\tfooter := plaintext[footerOffset : int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8]\n997\t\t\t\t\th.footerCache.Set(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag, footer)\n998\t\t\t\t}\n999\t\t\t}\n1000\t\t}\n1001\t}\n1002\t\n1003\t// checkConditionalRequest evaluates conditional headers and returns the appropriate\n1004\t// response status. Returns 0 if the request should proceed normally.\n1005\t// Supports: If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\n1006\tfunc checkConditionalRequest(r *http.Request, etag string, lastModified time.Time) int {\n1007\t\tifMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-Match\")\n1008\t\tifNoneMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-None-Match\")\n1009\t\tifModifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Modified-Since\")\n1010\t\tifUnmodifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Unmodified-Since\")\n1011\t\n1012\t\t// Normalize ETag (remove quotes if present for comparison)\n1013\t\tnormalizedETag := strings.Trim(etag, `\"`)\n1014\t\n1015\t\t// If-Match: Return 412 Precondition Failed if ETag doesn't match\n1016\t\tif ifMatch != \"\" {\n1017\t\t\t// If-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n1018\t\t\tif ifMatch == \"*\" {\n1019\t\t\t\t// Match any existing resource - proceed\n1020\t\t\t} else {\n1021\t\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n1022\t\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifMatch, \",\")\n1023\t\t\t\tmatched := false\n1024\t\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n1025\t\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n1026\t\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n1027\t\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n1028\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n1029\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1030\t\t\t\t\t}\n1031\t\t\t\t}\n1032\t\t\t\tif !matched {\n1033\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n1034\t\t\t\t}\n1035\t\t\t}\n1036\t\t}\n1037\t\n1038\t\t// If-Unmodified-Since: Return 412 Precondition Failed if modified since date\n1039\t\tif ifUnmodifiedSince != \"\" {\n1040\t\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifUnmodifiedSince); err == nil {\n1041\t\t\t\tif lastModified.After(t) {\n1042\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n1043\t\t\t\t}\n1044\t\t\t}\n1045\t\t}\n1046\t\n1047\t\t// If-None-Match: Return 304 Not Modified if ETag matches\n1048\t\tif ifNoneMatch != \"\" {\n1049\t\t\t// If-None-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n1050\t\t\tif ifNoneMatch == \"*\" {\n1051\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1052\t\t\t}\n1053\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n1054\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifNoneMatch, \",\")\n1055\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n1056\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n1057\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n1058\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n1059\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1060\t\t\t\t}\n1061\t\t\t}\n1062\t\t}\n1063\t\n1064\t\t// If-Modified-Since: Return 304 Not Modified if not modified since date\n1065\t\t// Only applies if If-None-Match is not present (per RFC 7232)\n1066\t\tif ifModifiedSince != \"\" && ifNoneMatch == \"\" {\n1067\t\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifModifiedSince); err == nil {\n1068\t\t\t\t// Use >= comparison per RFC 7232\n1069\t\t\t\tif !lastModified.After(t) {\n1070\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1071\t\t\t\t}\n1072\t\t\t}\n1073\t\t}\n1074\t\n1075\t\treturn 0\n1076\t}\n1077\t\n1078\t// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\n1079\tfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n1080\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n1081\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1082\t\t}\n1083\t\n1084\t\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n1085\t\n1086\t\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n1087\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n1088\t\t}\n1089\t\n1090\t\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n1091\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n1092\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1093\t\t}\n1094\t\n1095\t\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n1096\t\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n1097\t\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n1098\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1099\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1100\t\t\t}\n1101\t\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n1102\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1103\t\t} else {\n1104\t\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n1105\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1106\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1107\t\t\t}\n1108\t\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n1109\t\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1110\t\t\t} else {\n1111\t\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n1112\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1113\t\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1114\t\t\t\t}\n1115\t\t\t}\n1116\t\t}\n1117\t\n1118\t\t// Clamp end per RFC 7233: a range ending beyond the file size is satisfiable\n1119\t\t// as long as start is within bounds. DuckDB httpfs requests aligned blocks\n1120\t\t// (e.g. bytes=0-131071) regardless of actual file size.\n1121\t\tif end >= totalSize {\n1122\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1123\t\t}\n1124\t\n1125\t\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start {\n1126\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n1127\t\t}\n1128\t\n1129\t\treturn start, end, nil\n1130\t}\n1131\t\n1132\t// HeadObject handles S3 HeadObject.\n1133\tfunc (h *Handlers) HeadObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1134\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1135\t\n1136\t\t// Fast path: serve from the in-memory manifest index when available,\n1137\t\t// avoiding a B2 HeadObject round-trip entirely.\n1138\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1139\t\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, key); ok {\n1140\t\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, entry.ETag, entry.LastModified); status != 0 {\n1141\t\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n1142\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n1143\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1144\t\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n1145\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n1146\t\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n1147\t\t\t\t\t}\n1148\t\t\t\t\treturn\n1149\t\t\t\t}\n1150\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(entry.PlaintextSize, 10))\n1151\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", entry.ContentType)\n1152\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n1153\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1154\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1155\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1156\t\t\t\treturn\n1157\t\t\t}\n1158\t\t}\n1159\t\n1160\t\t// Manifest miss or disabled: fall back to a B2 HeadObject call.\n1161\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n1162\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n1163\t\tif err != nil {\n1164\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Object not found\", 404)\n1165\t\t\treturn\n1166\t\t}\n1167\t\n1168\t\t// Determine ETag and content info based on encryption status\n1169\t\tvar etag string\n1170\t\tvar contentLength int64\n1171\t\tvar contentType string\n1172\t\tif info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n1173\t\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n1174\t\t\t\tetag = am.ETag\n1175\t\t\t\tcontentLength = am.PlaintextSize\n1176\t\t\t\tcontentType = am.ContentType\n1177\t\t\t} else {\n1178\t\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n1179\t\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n1180\t\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n1181\t\t\t}\n1182\t\t} else {\n1183\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n1184\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n1185\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n1186\t\t}\n1187\t\n1188\t\t// Check conditional request headers\n1189\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, etag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n1190\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n1191\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1192\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1193\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n1194\t\t\t} else {\n1195\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n1196\t\t\t}\n1197\t\t\treturn\n1198\t\t}\n1199\t\n1200\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(contentLength, 10))\n1201\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", contentType)\n1202\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1203\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1204\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1205\t\n1206\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1207\t}\n1208\t\n1209\t// DeleteObject handles S3 DeleteObject.\n1210\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1211\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1212\t\n1213\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n1214\t\tif err := h.backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey); err != nil {\n1215\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to delete: %v\", err), 500)\n1216\t\t\treturn\n1217\t\t}\n1218\t\n1219\t\t// Remove from manifest\n1220\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1221\t\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n1222\t\t}\n1223\t\n1224\t\t// Invalidate metadata cache and list cache\n1225\t\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n1226\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1227\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n1228\t\t}\n1229\t\n1230\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n1231\t}\n1232\t\n1233\t// CopyObject handles S3 CopyObject with DEK re-wrapping for ARMOR-encrypted objects.\n1234\t// This supports:\n1235\t// - Renaming files (same bucket, different key)\n1236\t// - Copying files (potentially different bucket)\n1237\t// - Key rotation (re-wraps DEK with current MEK)\n1238\tfunc (h *Handlers) CopyObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dstBucket, dstKey string) {\n1239\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1240\t\n1241\t\t// Parse copy source header\n1242\t\tcopySource := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\")\n1243\t\tif copySource == \"\" {\n1244\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing x-amz-copy-source header\", 400)\n1245\t\t\treturn\n1246\t\t}\n1247\t\n1248\t\t// Parse source bucket and key\n1249\t\t// Format: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n1250\t\tsrcBucket, srcKey := parseCopySource(copySource)\n1251\t\tif srcBucket == \"\" || srcKey == \"\" {\n1252\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidCopySource\", \"Invalid copy source format\", 400)\n1253\t\t\treturn\n1254\t\t}\n1255\t\n1256\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n1257\t\tsrcPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(srcKey)\n1258\t\tdstPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(dstKey)\n1259\t\n1260\t\t// Get source object metadata\n1261\t\tsrcInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey)\n1262\t\tif err != nil {\n1263\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Source object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1264\t\t\treturn\n1265\t\t}\n1266\t\n1267\t\t// Check metadata directive\n1268\t\tmetadataDirective := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-metadata-directive\")\n1269\t\treplaceMetadata := metadataDirective == \"REPLACE\"\n1270\t\n1271\t\t// Build response XML structure\n1272\t\ttype CopyObjectResult struct {\n1273\t\t\tXMLName      xml.Name `xml:\"CopyObjectResult\"`\n1274\t\t\tLastModified string   `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n1275\t\t\tETag         string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n1276\t\t}\n1277\t\n1278\t\t// Handle ARMOR-encrypted objects\n1279\t\tif srcInfo.IsARMOREncrypted {\n1280\t\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n1281\t\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(srcInfo.Metadata)\n1282\t\t\tif !ok {\n1283\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n1284\t\t\t\treturn\n1285\t\t\t}\n1286\t\n1287\t\t\t// Get the source MEK using the key ID from metadata\n1288\t\t\tsrcMEK, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n1289\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1290\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get source decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1291\t\t\t\treturn\n1292\t\t\t}\n1293\t\n1294\t\t\t// Unwrap DEK with source MEK\n1295\t\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(srcMEK, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n1296\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1297\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1298\t\t\t\treturn\n1299\t\t\t}\n1300\t\n1301\t\t\t// Get the destination MEK for the target key\n1302\t\t\tdstMEK, dstKeyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(dstKey)\n1303\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1304\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get destination encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1305\t\t\t\treturn\n1306\t\t\t}\n1307\t\n1308\t\t\t// Re-wrap DEK with destination MEK (handles key rotation and cross-prefix copy)\n1309\t\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(dstMEK, dek)\n1310\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1311\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to re-wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1312\t\t\t\treturn\n1313\t\t\t}\n1314\t\n1315\t\t\t// Build new metadata with re-wrapped DEK and destination key ID\n1316\t\t\tnewMeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n1317\t\t\t\tVersion:       armorMeta.Version,\n1318\t\t\t\tBlockSize:     armorMeta.BlockSize,\n1319\t\t\t\tPlaintextSize: armorMeta.PlaintextSize,\n1320\t\t\t\tContentType:   armorMeta.ContentType,\n1321\t\t\t\tIV:            armorMeta.IV,\n1322\t\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n1323\t\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  armorMeta.PlaintextSHA,\n1324\t\t\t\tETag:          armorMeta.ETag,\n1325\t\t\t\tKeyID:         dstKeyID,\n1326\t\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n1327\t\n1328\t\t\t// Handle REPLACE directive - copy custom metadata headers from request\n1329\t\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n1330\t\t\t\t// Check for new content-type in request\n1331\t\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n1332\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-content-type\"] = ct\n1333\t\t\t\t}\n1334\t\t\t\t// Copy any additional custom headers from request\n1335\t\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n1336\t\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") && !strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-Armor-\") {\n1337\t\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n1338\t\t\t\t\t}\n1339\t\t\t\t}\n1340\t\t\t}\n1341\t\n1342\t\t\t// Perform server-side copy with updated metadata\n1343\t\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, newMeta, true); err != nil {\n1344\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n1345\t\t\t\treturn\n1346\t\t\t}\n1347\t\n1348\t\t\t// Record provenance for the copy\n1349\t\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(dstKey) {\n1350\t\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, dstKey, armorMeta.PlaintextSHA, \"copy\")\n1351\t\t\t}\n1352\t\n1353\t\t\t// Invalidate cache for destination\n1354\t\t\th.cache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n1355\t\t\th.footerCache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n1356\t\n1357\t\t\t// Return success response\n1358\t\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n1359\t\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1360\t\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag),\n1361\t\t\t}\n1362\t\n1363\t\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1364\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1365\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1366\t\t\t\treturn\n1367\t\t\t}\n1368\t\n1369\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1370\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1371\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1372\t\t\tw.Write(output)\n1373\t\t\treturn\n1374\t\t}\n1375\t\n1376\t\t// Non-ARMOR object - pass through copy\n1377\t\tvar meta map[string]string\n1378\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n1379\t\t\tmeta = make(map[string]string)\n1380\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n1381\t\t\t\tmeta[\"Content-Type\"] = ct\n1382\t\t\t}\n1383\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n1384\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") {\n1385\t\t\t\t\tmeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n1386\t\t\t\t}\n1387\t\t\t}\n1388\t\t}\n1389\t\n1390\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, meta, replaceMetadata); err != nil {\n1391\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n1392\t\t\treturn\n1393\t\t}\n1394\t\n1395\t\t// Get the destination object info for ETag\n1396\t\tdstInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey)\n1397\t\tif err != nil {\n1398\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to get destination info\", 500)\n1399\t\t\treturn\n1400\t\t}\n1401\t\n1402\t\t// Return success response\n1403\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n1404\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1405\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, dstInfo.ETag),\n1406\t\t}\n1407\t\n1408\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1409\t\tif err != nil {\n1410\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1411\t\t\treturn\n1412\t\t}\n1413\t\n1414\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1415\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1416\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1417\t\tw.Write(output)\n1418\t}\n1419\t\n1420\t// parseCopySource parses the x-amz-copy-source header value.\n1421\t// Supports formats: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n1422\tfunc parseCopySource(copySource string) (bucket, key string) {\n1423\t\t// Remove leading slash if present\n1424\t\tcopySource = strings.TrimPrefix(copySource, \"/\")\n1425\t\n1426\t\t// URL decode the key portion (keys may contain special characters)\n1427\t\tif idx := strings.Index(copySource, \"/\"); idx != -1 {\n1428\t\t\tbucket = copySource[:idx]\n1429\t\t\tkey = copySource[idx+1:]\n1430\t\t\t// URL decode the key\n1431\t\t\tif decoded, err := url.QueryUnescape(key); err == nil {\n1432\t\t\t\tkey = decoded\n1433\t\t\t}\n1434\t\t}\n1435\t\n1436\t\treturn bucket, key\n1437\t}\n1438\t\n1439\t// enrichPlaintextSizes replaces encrypted on-disk sizes with plaintext sizes for\n1440\t// ARMOR-encrypted objects. B2's ListObjectsV2 response includes the encrypted\n1441\t// object size; we resolve the plaintext size using the metadata cache (if warm)\n1442\t// or a parallel HeadObject call per object (on cache miss). Each errgroup goroutine\n1443\t// writes to a distinct slice index so no mutex is needed.\n1444\tfunc (h *Handlers) enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx context.Context, bucket string, result *backend.ListResult) {\n1445\t\teg, egCtx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n1446\t\tfor i := range result.Objects {\n1447\t\t\ti := i\n1448\t\t\tkey := result.Objects[i].Key\n1449\t\t\tif am, ok := h.cache.Get(bucket, key); ok {\n1450\t\t\t\tif am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n1451\t\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = am.PlaintextSize\n1452\t\t\t\t}\n1453\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1454\t\t\t}\n1455\t\t\teg.Go(func() error {\n1456\t\t\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(egCtx, bucket, key)\n1457\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1458\t\t\t\t\treturn nil // non-fatal: keep encrypted size rather than failing the listing\n1459\t\t\t\t}\n1460\t\t\t\tif info.Size > 0 {\n1461\t\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = info.Size\n1462\t\t\t\t}\n1463\t\t\t\treturn nil\n1464\t\t\t})\n1465\t\t}\n1466\t\teg.Wait() //nolint:errcheck\n1467\t}\n1468\t\n1469\t// ListObjectsV2 handles S3 ListObjectsV2.\n1470\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListObjectsV2(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1471\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1472\t\n1473\t\tprefix := r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n1474\t\tdelimiter := r.URL.Query().Get(\"delimiter\")\n1475\t\tcontToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"continuation-token\")\n1476\t\tmaxKeys := 1000\n1477\t\tif mk := r.URL.Query().Get(\"max-keys\"); mk != \"\" {\n1478\t\t\tif v, err := strconv.Atoi(mk); err == nil && v > 0 {\n1479\t\t\t\tmaxKeys = v\n1480\t\t\t}\n1481\t\t}\n1482\t\n1483\t\t// Apply the configured prefix to the prefix parameter for backend operations.\n1484\t\t// When ARMOR_PREFIX is set, the backend stores all keys with the prefix prepended,\n1485\t\t// but clients don't know about it. We need to prepend the prefix to the client's\n1486\t\t// requested prefix so the backend finds the right objects.\n1487\t\tbackendPrefix := h.applyPrefix(prefix)\n1488\t\n1489\t\tvar result *backend.ListResult\n1490\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1491\t\t\tif cached, ok := h.listCache.Get(bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken); ok {\n1492\t\t\t\tresult = cached\n1493\t\t\t}\n1494\t\t}\n1495\t\tif result == nil {\n1496\t\t\tvar err error\n1497\t\t\tresult, err = h.backend.List(ctx, bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, contToken, maxKeys)\n1498\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1499\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list: %v\", err), 500)\n1500\t\t\t\treturn\n1501\t\t\t}\n1502\t\t\t// Resolve plaintext sizes for ARMOR-encrypted objects before caching.\n1503\t\t\t// B2 ListObjectsV2 returns encrypted (on-disk) sizes; clients like DuckDB\n1504\t\t\t// use these sizes to compute byte-range offsets, so they must reflect the\n1505\t\t\t// plaintext length.\n1506\t\t\th.enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx, bucket, result)\n1507\t\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1508\t\t\t\th.listCache.Set(bucket, prefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken, result)\n1509\t\t\t}\n1510\t\t}\n1511\t\n1512\t\t// Build XML response\n1513\t\ttype Contents struct {\n1514\t\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1515\t\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n1516\t\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n1517\t\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n1518\t\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n1519\t\t}\n1520\t\n1521\t\t// S3 spec: each CommonPrefixes entry is a separate wrapper element with one\n1522\t\t// Prefix child. Using []string with \"Outer>Inner\" produces an empty\n1523\t\t// <CommonPrefixes/> when the slice is nil, which the AWS SDK v1 parses as a\n1524\t\t// slice with one nil element — causing nil-pointer crashes in clients like\n1525\t\t// litestream. Using a dedicated struct + omitempty avoids this.\n1526\t\ttype CommonPrefix struct {\n1527\t\t\tPrefix string `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n1528\t\t}\n1529\t\n1530\t\ttype ListBucketResult struct {\n1531\t\t\tXMLName               xml.Name       `xml:\"ListBucketResult\"`\n1532\t\t\tXmlns                 string         `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1533\t\t\tName                  string         `xml:\"Name\"`\n1534\t\t\tPrefix                string         `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n1535\t\t\tDelimiter             string         `xml:\"Delimiter,omitempty\"`\n1536\t\t\tMaxKeys               int            `xml:\"MaxKeys\"`\n1537\t\t\tIsTruncated           bool           `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n1538\t\t\tNextContinuationToken string         `xml:\"NextContinuationToken,omitempty\"`\n1539\t\t\tContents              []Contents\n1540\t\t\tCommonPrefixes        []CommonPrefix `xml:\"CommonPrefixes,omitempty\"`\n1541\t\t}\n1542\t\n1543\t\tresp := ListBucketResult{\n1544\t\t\tXmlns:                 \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1545\t\t\tName:                  bucket,\n1546\t\t\tPrefix:                prefix,\n1547\t\t\tDelimiter:             delimiter,\n1548\t\t\tMaxKeys:               maxKeys,\n1549\t\t\tIsTruncated:           result.IsTruncated,\n1550\t\t\tNextContinuationToken: result.NextToken,\n1551\t\t}\n1552\t\n1553\t\tfor _, obj := range result.Objects {\n1554\t\t\t// Strip the configured prefix from object keys before returning to client.\n1555\t\t\t// Clients don't know about the prefix, so we need to remove it from the keys.\n1556\t\t\tstrippedKey := h.stripPrefix(obj.Key)\n1557\t\t\tresp.Contents = append(resp.Contents, Contents{\n1558\t\t\t\tKey:          strippedKey,\n1559\t\t\t\tLastModified: obj.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1560\t\t\t\tETag: {\n1567\t\t\t// Strip the configured prefix from common prefixes before returning to client.\n1568\t\t\t// Common prefixes are used for directory-like listings with delimiters.\n1569\t\t\tstrippedPrefix := h.stripPrefixFromCommonPrefix(p)\n1570\t\t\tresp.CommonPrefixes = append(resp.CommonPrefixes, CommonPrefix{Prefix: strippedPrefix})\n1571\t\t}\n1572\t\n1573\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n1574\t\tif err != nil {\n1575\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1576\t\t\treturn\n1577\t\t}\n1578\t\n1579\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1580\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1581\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1582\t\tw.Write(output)\n1583\t}\n1584\t\n1585\t// Stub implementations for other operations\n1586\t\n1587\t// HeadBucket handles S3 HeadBucket.\n1588\tfunc (h *Handlers) contains XML with a list of objects to delete.\n1683\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObjects(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1684\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1685\t\n1686\t\t// Parse the DeleteObjects request XML\n1687\t\ttype Object struct {\n1688\t\t\tKey string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1689\t\t}\n1690\t\n1691\t\ttype DeleteRequest struct {\n1692\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n1700\t\tif err != nil {\n1701\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n1702\t\t\treturn\n1703\t\t}\n1704\t\n1705\t\tif err := CreateMultipartUpload handles S3 CreateMultipartUpload with ARMOR encryption.\n1833\t// It generates a DEK and IV, wraps the DEK, and stores the state in B2.\n1834\tfunc (h *Handlers) CreateMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1835\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1836\t\n1837\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n1838\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n1839\t\tif err != nil {\n1840\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1841\t\t\treturn\n1842\t\t}\n1843\t\n1844\t\t// Generate DEK and IV for this upload\n1845\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n1846\t\tif err != nil {\n1847\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1848\t\t\treturn\n1849\t\t}\n1850\t\n1851\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n1852\t\tif err != nil {\n1853\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n1854\t\t\treturn\n1855\t\t}\n1856\t\n1857\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n1858\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n1859\t\tif err != nil {\n1860\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1861\t\t\treturn\n1862\t\t}\n1863\t\n1864\t\t// Get content type\n1865\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n1866\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n1867\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n1868\t\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t\t// Initiate multipart upload with B2 (no ARMOR metadata yet - that comes on completion)\n1871\t\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, nil)\n1872\t\tif err != nil {\n1873\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n1874\t\t\treturn\n1875\t\t}\n1876\t\n1877\t\t// Save multipart state to B2\n1878\t\tstate := &backend.MultipartState{\n1879\t\t\tUploadID:       uploadID,\n1880\t\t\tBucket:         bucket,\n1881\t\t\tKey:            key,\n1882\t\t\tIV:             iv,\n1883\t\t\tWrappedDEK:     wrappedDEK,\n1884\t\t\tBlockSize:      h.config.BlockSize,\n1885\t\t\tCreated:        time.Now(),\n1886\t\t\tContentType:    contentType,\n1887\t\t\tKeyID:          keyID,\n1888\t\t\tEncryptedBytes: 0,\n1889\t\t\tPartHMACs:      make(map[int]string),\n1890\t\t\tPartSizes:      make(map[int]int64),\n1891\t\t}\n1892\t\n1893\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1894\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n1895\t\t\t// Try to abort the upload on state save failure\n1896\t\t\th.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n1897\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n1898\t\t\treturn\n1899\t\t}\n1900\t\n1901\t\t// Build XML response\n1902\t\ttype InitiateMultipartUploadResult struct {\n1903\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"InitiateMultipartUploadResult\"`\n1904\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1905\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n1906\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n1907\t\t\tUploadID string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n1908\t\t}\n1909\t\n1910\t\tresult := InitiateMultipartUploadResult{\n1911\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1912\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n1913\t\t\tKey:      key,\n1914\t\t\tUploadID: uploadID,\n1915\t\t}\n1916\t\n1917\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1918\t\tif err != nil {\n1919\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1920\t\t\treturn\n1921\t\t}\n1922\t\n1923\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1924\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1925\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1926\t\tw.Write(output)\n1927\t}\n1928\t\n1929\t// UploadPart handles S3 UploadPart with encryption.\n1930\t// Each part is encrypted with a CTR counter offset based on cumulative encrypted bytes.\n1931\tfunc (h *Handlers) UploadPart(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n1932\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1933\t\n1934\t\t// Parse part number\n1935\t\tpartNumberStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\")\n1936\t\tif partNumberStr == \"\" {\n1937\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing partNumber\", 400)\n1938\t\t\treturn\n1939\t\t}\n1940\t\tpartNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(partNumberStr, 10, 32)\n1941\t\tif err != nil || partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n1942\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Invalid partNumber\", 400)\n1943\t\t\treturn\n1944\t\t}\n1945\t\n1946\t\t// Load multipart state\n1947\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1948\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n1949\t\tif err != nil {\n1950\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1951\t\t\treturn\n1952\t\t}\n1953\t\n1954\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n1955\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n1956\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n1957\t\t\treturn\n1958\t\t}\n1959\t\n1960\t\t// Read plaintext part\n1961\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n1962\t\tif err != nil {\n1963\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n1964\t\t\treturn\n1965\t\t}\n1966\t\n1967\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n1968\t\n1969\t\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n1970\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(state.KeyID)\n1971\t\tif err != nil {\n1972\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1973\t\t\treturn\n1974\t\t}\n1975\t\n1976\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n1977\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, state.WrappedDEK)\n1978\t\tif err != nil {\n1979\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1980\t\t\treturn\n1981\t\t}\n1982\t\n1983\t\t// Calculate starting block index for CTR counter\n1984\t\t// Each block is blockSize bytes, so counter = encryptedBytes / blockSize\n1985\t\tstartBlockIndex := uint32(state.EncryptedBytes / int64(state.BlockSize))\n1986\t\n1987\t\t// Create encryptor with the part's starting counter\n1988\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptorWithCounter(dek, state.IV, state.BlockSize, startBlockIndex)\n1989\t\tif err != nil {\n1990\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n1991\t\t\treturn\n1992\t\t}\n1993\t\n1994\t\t// Encrypt the part\n1995\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n1996\t\tif err != nil {\n1997\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n1998\t\t\treturn\n1999\t\t}\n2000\t\n2001\t\t// Derive HMAC key\n2002\t\thmacKey := crypto.DeriveHMACKey(dek)\n2003\t\n2004\t\t// Compute block HMACs\n2005\t\tblockHMACs, err := backend.ComputeBlockHMACs(encrypted, state.BlockSize, hmacKey, startBlockIndex)\n2006\t\tif err != nil {\n2007\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compute HMACs: %v\", err), 500)\n2008\t\t\treturn\n2009\t\t}\n2010\t\n2011\t\t// Upload encrypted part to B2\n2012\t\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, int32(partNumber), bytes.NewReader(encrypted), int64(len(encrypted)))\n2013\t\tif err != nil {\n2014\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n2015\t\t\treturn\n2016\t\t}\n2017\t\n2018\t\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n2019\t\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n2020\t\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n2021\t\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n2022\t\n2023\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n2024\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n2025\t\t\treturn\n2026\t\t}\n2027\t\n2028\t\t// Return ETag - suppress unused variable warning\n2029\t\t_ = hmacTable\n2030\t\n2031\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n2032\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2033\t}\n2034\t\n2035\t// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n2036\t// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\n2037\tfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2038\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2039\t\n2040\t\t// Load multipart state\n2041\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2042\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2043\t\tif err != nil {\n2044\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2045\t\t\treturn\n2046\t\t}\n2047\t\n2048\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2049\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2050\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2051\t\t\treturn\n2052\t\t}\n2053\t\n2054\t\t// Parse the CompleteMultipartUpload request XML\n2055\t\ttype Part struct {\n2056\t\t\tPartNumber int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n2057\t\t\tETag       string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2058\t\t}\n2059\t\n2060\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadReq struct {\n2061\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n2062\t\t\tParts   []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n2063\t\t}\n2064\t\n2065\t\tvar completeReq CompleteMultipartUploadReq\n2066\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n2067\t\tif err != nil {\n2068\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n2069\t\t\treturn\n2070\t\t}\n2071\t\n2072\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &completeReq); err != nil {\n2073\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n2074\t\t\treturn\n2075\t\t}\n2076\t\n2077\t\tif len(completeReq.Parts) == 0 {\n2078\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"No parts specified\", 400)\n2079\t\t\treturn\n2080\t\t}\n2081\t\n2082\t\t// Convert to backend.CompletedPart\n2083\t\tparts := make([]backend.CompletedPart, len(completeReq.Parts))\n2084\t\tfor i, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2085\t\t\tparts[i] = backend.CompletedPart{\n2086\t\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(p.PartNumber),\n2087\t\t\t\tETag:       p.ETag,\n2088\t\t\t}\n2089\t\t}\n2090\t\n2091\t\t// Calculate total plaintext size\n2092\t\tvar totalPlaintextSize int64\n2093\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2094\t\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2095\t\t\t\ttotalPlaintextSize += size\n2096\t\t\t}\n2097\t\t}\n2098\t\n2099\t\t// Assemble all block HMACs in order\n2100\t\tvar allBlockHMACs [][]byte\n2101\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2102\t\t\tif hmacsBase64, ok := state.PartHMACs[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2103\t\t\t\thmacs, err := backend.DecodeHMACFromBase64(hmacsBase64)\n2104\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2105\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode HMACs for part %d: %v\", p.PartNumber, err), 500)\n2106\t\t\t\t\treturn\n2107\t\t\t\t}\n2108\t\t\t\tallBlockHMACs = append(allBlockHMACs, hmacs...)\n2109\t\t\t}\n2110\t\t}\n2111\t\n2112\t\t// Complete the multipart upload in B2\n2113\t\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n2114\t\tif err != nil {\n2115\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to complete multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n2116\t\t\treturn\n2117\t\t}\n2118\t\n2119\t\t// Store HMAC table as sidecar\n2120\t\tif err := manager.SaveHMACTable(ctx, key, allBlockHMACs, state.BlockSize); err != nil {\n2121\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n2122\t\t\treturn\n2123\t\t}\n2124\t\n2125\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256 (we don't have the full plaintext, so use a placeholder)\n2126\t\t// In a full implementation, we'd track SHA during upload\n2127\t\tplaintextSHA := sha256.Sum256([]byte{})\n2128\t\n2129\t\t// Build ARMOR metadata and update via CopyObject\n2130\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n2131\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n2132\t\t\tBlockSize:     state.BlockSize,\n2133\t\t\tPlaintextSize: totalPlaintextSize,\n2134\t\t\tContentType:   state.ContentType,\n2135\t\t\tIV:            state.IV,\n2136\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    state.WrappedDEK,\n2137\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n2138\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n2139\t\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n2140\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n2141\t\n2142\t\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n2143\t\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n2144\t\n2145\t\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n2146\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n2147\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n2148\t\t\treturn\n2149\t\t}\n2150\t\n2151\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2152\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2153\t\n2154\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n2155\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n2156\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, totalPlaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), state.IV, state.WrappedDEK, state.BlockSize, state.ContentType, etag)\n2157\t\t}\n2158\t\n2159\t\t// Record provenance for the multipart upload\n2160\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n2161\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), \"multipart\")\n2162\t\t}\n2163\t\n2164\t\t// Build XML response\n2165\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadResult struct {\n2166\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUploadResult\"`\n2167\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2168\t\t\tLocation string   `xml:\"Location\"`\n2169\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n2170\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n2171\t\t\tETag     string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2172\t\t}\n2173\t\n2174\t\tresult := CompleteMultipartUploadResult{\n2175\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2176\t\t\tLocation: fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s\", bucket, key),\n2177\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n2178\t\t\tKey:      key,\n2179\t\t\tETag:     etag,\n2180\t\t}\n2181\t\n2182\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n2183\t\tif err != nil {\n2184\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n2185\t\t\treturn\n2186\t\t}\n2187\t\n2188\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n2189\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2190\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n2191\t\tw.Write(output)\n2192\t}\n2193\t\n2194\t// AbortMultipartUpload handles S3 AbortMultipartUpload.\n2195\t// It deletes the multipart state and forwards the abort to B2.\n2196\tfunc (h *Handlers) AbortMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2197\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2198\t\n2199\t\t// Load multipart state to verify it exists\n2200\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2201\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2202\t\tif err != nil {\n2203\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2204\t\t\treturn\n2205\t\t}\n2206\t\n2207\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2208\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2209\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2210\t\t\treturn\n2211\t\t}\n2212\t\n2213\t\t// Forward abort to B2\n2214\t\tif err := h.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID); err != nil {\n2215\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to abort multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n2216\t\t\treturn\n2217\t\t}\n2218\t\n2219\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2220\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2221\t\n2222\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n2223\t}\n2224\t\n2225\t// ListParts handles S3 ListParts operation.\n2226\t// It forwards to B2 and adjusts part sizes to plaintext sizes.\n2227\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListParts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2228\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2229\t\n2230\t\t// Load multipart state to get plaintext sizes\n2231\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2232\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2233\t\tif err != nil {\n2234\t\t\th.writeError(w, := h.backend.ListParts(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n2246\t\tif err != nil {\n2247\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list parts: %v\", err),            xml.Name `xml:\"ListPartsResult\"`\n2261\t\t\tXmlns                string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2262\t\t\tBucket               string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n2263\t\t\tKey \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2275\t\t\tBucket:               bucket,\n2276\t\t\tKey:                  key,\n2277\t\t\tUploadID:             uploadID,\n2278\t\t\tStorageClass: \"application/xml\")\n2306\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2307\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n2308\t\tw.Write(output)\n2309\t}\n2310\t\n2311\t// ListMultipartUploads handles S3 ListMultipartUploads operation.\n2312\t// It forwards directly to B2 (passthrough operation).\n2313\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListMultipartUploads(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n2314\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2315\t\n2316\t\tresult, err := h.backend.ListMultipartUploads(ctx, bucket)\n2317\t\tif err != nil {\n2318\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list multipart uploads: %v\", err), 500)\n2319\t\t\treturn\n2320\t\t}\n2321\t\n2322\t\t// Build XML response\n2323\t\ttype Upload struct {\n2324\t\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n2325\t\t\tUploadID     string `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n2326\t\t\tInitiator    string `xml:\"Initiator>ID\"`\n2327\t\t\tOwner        string {\n2333\t\t\tXMLName            xml.Name `xml:\"ListMultipartUploadsResult\"`\n2334\t\t\tXmlns              string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2335\t\t\tBucket             string Package handlers implements S3 operation handlers for ARMOR.\npackage handlers\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/cipher\"\n\t\"crypto/hmac\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/binary\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"path\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/crypto\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/keymanager\"\n)\n\n//
