encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// CreateMultipartUpload handles S3 CreateMultipartUpload with ARMOR encryption.\n// It generates a DEK and IV, wraps the DEK, and stores the state in B2.\nfunc (h *Handlers) CreateMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate DEK and IV for this upload\n\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get content type\n\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Initiate multipart upload with B2 (no ARMOR metadata yet - that comes on completion)\n\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, nil)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Save multipart state to B2\n\tstate := &backend.MultipartState{\n\t\tUploadID:       uploadID,\n\t\tBucket:         bucket,\n\t\tKey:            key,\n\t\tIV:             iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:     wrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:      h.config.BlockSize,\n\t\tCreated:        time.Now(),\n\t\tContentType:    contentType,\n\t\tKeyID:          keyID,\n\t\tEncryptedBytes: 0,\n\t\tPartHMACs:      make(map[int]string),\n\t\tPartSizes:      make(map[int]int64),\n\t}\n\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n\t\t// Try to abort the upload on state save failure\n\t\th.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype InitiateMultipartUploadResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"InitiateMultipartUploadResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tUploadID string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresult := InitiateMultipartUploadResult{\n\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n\t\tKey:      key,\n\t\tUploadID: uploadID,\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// UploadPart handles S3 UploadPart with encryption.\n// Each part is encrypted with a CTR counter offset based on cumulative encrypted bytes.\nfunc (h *Handlers) UploadPart(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Parse part number\n\tpartNumberStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\")\n\tif partNumberStr == \"\" {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing partNumber\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tpartNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(partNumberStr, 10, 32)\n\tif err != nil || partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Invalid partNumber\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Load multipart state\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Read plaintext part\n\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(state.KeyID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, state.WrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate starting block index for CTR counter\n\t// Each block is blockSize bytes, so counter = encryptedBytes / blockSize\n\tstartBlockIndex := uint32(state.EncryptedBytes / int64(state.BlockSize))\n\n\t// Create encryptor with the part's starting counter\n\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptorWithCounter(dek, state.IV, state.BlockSize, startBlockIndex)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Encrypt the part\n\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Derive HMAC key\n\thmacKey := crypto.DeriveHMACKey(dek)\n\n\t// Compute block HMACs\n\tblockHMACs, err := backend.ComputeBlockHMACs(encrypted, state.BlockSize, hmacKey, startBlockIndex)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compute HMACs: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Upload encrypted part to B2\n\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, int32(partNumber), bytes.NewReader(encrypted), int64(len(encrypted)))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n\n\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Return ETag - suppress unused variable warning\n\t_ = hmacTable\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n}\n\n// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\nfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the CompleteMultipartUpload request XML\n\ttype Part struct {\n\t\tPartNumber int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n\t\tETag       string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadReq struct {\n\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n\t\tParts   []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n\t}\n\n\tvar completeReq CompleteMultipartUploadReq\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &completeReq); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif len(completeReq.Parts) == 0 {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"No parts specified\", 400)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert to backend.CompletedPart\n\tparts := make([]backend.CompletedPart, len(completeReq.Parts))\n\tfor i, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n\t\tparts[i] = backend.CompletedPart{\n\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(p.PartNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:       p.ETag,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate total plaintext size\n\tvar totalPlaintextSize int64\n\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n\t\t\ttotalPlaintextSize += size\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Assemble all block HMACs in order\n\tvar allBlockHMACs [][]byte\n\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n\t\tif hmacsBase64, ok := state.PartHMACs[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n\t\t\thmacs, err := backend.DecodeHMACFromBase64(hmacsBase64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode HMACs for part %d: %v\", p.PartNumber, err), 500)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tallBlockHMACs = append(allBlockHMACs, hmacs...)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Complete the multipart upload in B2\n\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to complete multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Store HMAC table as sidecar\n\tif err := manager.SaveHMACTable(ctx, key, allBlockHMACs, state.BlockSize); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256 (we don't have the full plaintext, so use a placeholder)\n\t// In a full implementation, we'd track SHA during upload\n\tplaintextSHA := sha256.Sum256([]byte{})\n\n\t// Build ARMOR metadata and update via CopyObject\n\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n\t\tVersion:       1,\n\t\tBlockSize:     state.BlockSize,\n\t\tPlaintextSize: totalPlaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   state.ContentType,\n\t\tIV:            state.IV,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    state.WrappedDEK,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n\t\tETag:          etag,\n\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n\t}).ToMetadata()\n\n\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n\n\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart state\n\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n\n\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n\tif h.manifest != nil {\n\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, totalPlaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), state.IV, state.WrappedDEK, state.BlockSize, state.ContentType, etag)\n\t}\n\n\t// Record provenance for the multipart upload\n\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), \"multipart\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUploadResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tLocation string   `xml:\"Location\"`\n\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tETag     string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresult := CompleteMultipartUploadResult{\n\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tLocation: fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s\", bucket, key),\n\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n\t\tKey:      key,\n\t\tETag:     etag,\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// AbortMultipartUpload handles S3 AbortMultipartUpload.\n// It deletes the multipart state and forwards the abort to B2.\nfunc (h *Handlers) AbortMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state to verify it exists\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Forward abort to B2\n\tif err := h.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID); err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to abort multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up multipart state\n\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n}\n\n// ListParts handles S3 ListParts operation.\n// It forwards to B2 and adjusts part sizes to plaintext sizes.\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListParts(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\t// Load multipart state to get plaintext sizes\n\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify bucket and key match\n\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Forward to B2\n\tresult, err := h.backend.ListParts(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list parts: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response with plaintext sizes\n\ttype Part struct {\n\t\tPartNumber   int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListPartsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName              xml.Name `xml:\"ListPartsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns                string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tBucket               string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKey                  string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tUploadID             string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t\tStorageClass         string   `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n\t\tPartNumberMarker     int      `xml:\"PartNumberMarker\"`\n\t\tNextPartNumberMarker int      `xml:\"NextPartNumberMarker\"`\n\t\tMaxParts             int      `xml:\"MaxParts\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated          bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tParts                []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListPartsResult{\n\t\tXmlns:                \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tBucket:               bucket,\n\t\tKey:                  key,\n\t\tUploadID:             uploadID,\n\t\tStorageClass:         \"STANDARD\",\n\t\tPartNumberMarker:     result.NextPartNumberMarker,\n\t\tNextPartNumberMarker: result.NextPartNumberMarker,\n\t\tIsTruncated:          result.IsTruncated,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range result.Parts {\n\t\t// Use plaintext size from state if available, otherwise use reported size\n\t\tplaintextSize := part.Size\n\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[int(part.PartNumber)]; ok {\n\t\t\tplaintextSize = size\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp.Parts = append(resp.Parts, Part{\n\t\t\tPartNumber:   int(part.PartNumber),\n\t\t\tETag:         part.ETag,\n\t\t\tSize:         plaintextSize,\n\t\t\tLastModified: part.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// ListMultipartUploads handles S3 ListMultipartUploads operation.\n// It forwards directly to B2 (passthrough operation).\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListMultipartUploads(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tresult, err := h.backend.ListMultipartUploads(ctx, bucket)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list multipart uploads: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype Upload struct {\n\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tUploadID     string `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n\t\tInitiator    string `xml:\"Initiator>ID\"`\n\t\tOwner        string `xml:\"Owner>ID\"`\n\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n\t\tInitiated    string `xml:\"Initiated\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListMultipartUploadsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName            xml.Name `xml:\"ListMultipartUploadsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns              string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tBucket             string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n\t\tKeyMarker          string   `xml:\"KeyMarker\"`\n\t\tUploadIDMarker     string   `xml:\"UploadIdMarker\"`\n\t\tNextKeyMarker      string   `xml:\"NextKeyMarker\"`\n\t\tNextUploadIDMarker string   `xml:\"NextUploadIdMarker\"`\n\t\tMaxUploads         int      `xml:\"MaxUploads\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated        bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tUploads            []Upload `xml:\"Upload\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListMultipartUploadsResult{\n\t\tXmlns:              \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tBucket:             bucket,\n\t\tKeyMarker:          r.URL.Query().Get(\"key-marker\"),\n\t\tUploadIDMarker:     r.URL.Query().Get(\"upload-id-marker\"),\n\t\tNextKeyMarker:      result.NextKeyMarker,\n\t\tNextUploadIDMarker: result.NextUploadIDMarker,\n\t\tIsTruncated:        result.IsTruncated,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, upload := range result.Uploads {\n\t\tresp.Uploads = append(resp.Uploads, Upload{\n\t\t\tKey:          upload.Key,\n\t\t\tUploadID:     upload.UploadID,\n\t\t\tInitiator:    \"armor\",\n\t\t\tOwner:        \"armor\",\n\t\t\tStorageClass: \"STANDARD\",\n\t\t\tInitiated:    upload.Initiated.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n\tw.Write(output)\n}\n\n// ListObjectVersions handles S3 ListObjectVersions operation.\n// It lists all versions of objects in a bucket, For ARMOR-encrypted objects,\n// it retrieves per-version metadata to provide plaintext sizes.\nfunc (h *Handlers) ListObjectVersions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tprefix := r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n\tdelimiter := r.URL.Query().Get(\"delimiter\")\n\tkeyMarker := r.URL.Query().Get(\"key-marker\")\n\tversionIDMarker := r.URL.Query().Get(\"version-id-marker\")\n\tmaxKeys := 1000\n\tif mk := r.URL.Query().Get(\"max-keys\"); mk != \"\" {\n\t\tif v, err := strconv.Atoi(mk); err == nil && v > 0 {\n\t\t\tmaxKeys = v\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult, err := h.backend.ListObjectVersions(ctx, bucket, prefix, delimiter, keyMarker, versionIDMarker, maxKeys)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list object versions: %v\", err), 500)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build XML response\n\ttype Version struct {\n\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n\t\tVersionID    string `xml:\"VersionId\"`\n\t\tIsLatest     bool   `xml:\"IsLatest\"`\n\t\tIsDeleteMarker bool `xml:\"IsDeleteMarker,omitempty\"`\n\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag,omitempty\"`\n\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size,omitempty\"`\n\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass,omitempty\"`\n\t}\n\n\ttype ListVersionsResult struct {\n\t\tXMLName               xml.Name `xml:\"ListVersionsResult\"`\n\t\tXmlns                 string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n\t\tName                  string   `xml:\"Name\"`\n\t\tPrefix                string   `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n\t\tDelimiter             string   `xml:\"Delimiter,omitempty\"`\n\t\tMaxKeys               int      `xml:\"MaxKeys\"`\n\t\tIsTruncated           bool     `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n\t\tKeyMarker             string   `xml:\"KeyMarker\"`\n\t\tVersionIDMarker       string   `xml:\"VersionIdMarker\"`\n\t\tNextKeyMarker         string   `xml:\"NextKeyMarker\"`\n\t\tNextVersionIDMarker   string   `xml:\"NextVersionIdMarker\"`\n\t\tVersions              []Version `xml:\"Version\"`\n\t\tCommonPrefixes       []string `xml:\"CommonPrefixes>Prefix\"`\n\t}\n\n\tresp := ListVersionsResult{\n\t\tXmlns:               \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n\t\tName:                bucket,\n\t\tPrefix:              prefix,\n\t\tDelimiter:           delimiter,\n\t\tMaxKeys:             maxKeys,\n\t\tIsTruncated:         result.IsTruncated,\n\t\tKeyMarker:           keyMarker,\n\t\tVersionIDMarker:     versionIDMarker,\n\t\tNextKeyMarker:       result.NextKeyMarker,\n\t\tNextVersionIDMarker: result.NextVersionIDMarker,\n\t}\n\n\t// Process versions and retrieve per-version metadata for ARMOR objects\n\tfor _, version := range result.Versions {\n\t\tv := Version{\n\t\t\tKey:            version.Key,\n\t\t\tVersionID:      version.VersionID,\n\t\t\tIsLatest:       version.IsLatest,\n\t\t\tIsDeleteMarker: version.IsDeleteMarker,\n\t\t\tLastModified:   version.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !version.IsDeleteMarker {\n\t\t\t// For the latest version, try the manifest index first to avoid a\n\t\t\t// per-version HeadObject B2 API call. The manifest tracks current\n\t\t\t// state only, so this optimisation applies only to IsLatest.\n\t\t\tmanifestHit := false\n\t\t\tif h.manifest != nil && version.IsLatest {\n\t\t\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, version.Key); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tv.Size = entry.PlaintextSize\n\t\t\t\t\tv.ETag = fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag)\n\t\t\t\t\tv.StorageClass = \"STANDARD\"\n\t\t\t\t\tmanifestHit = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !manifestHit {\n\t\t\t\t// Fall back to a per-version HeadObject call (non-latest versions\n\t\t\t\t// or manifest miss).\n\t\t\t\tif info, err := h.backend.HeadVersion(ctx, bucket, version.Key, version.VersionID); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok main() {\n\t// Load configuration\n\tcfg, err := config.Load()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlogging.Fatalf(\"failed to load configuration: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create logger with configuration\n\tlogger time.Second,\n\t\tWriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,\n\t}\n\n\t// Start canary monitor\n\tsrv.StartCanary(context.Background())\n\n\t// Start servers in goroutines\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tlogger.Infof(\"S3 {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260714141527c69cf4ec4ed44d1a","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_10c3f16e7c444c95984ac3c7","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-18etvh-header-handling-analysis.md","content":"# ARMOR Header Handling Architecture\n\n**Date:** 2026-07-14  \n**Task:** bf-18etvh - Examine current header handling implementation  \n**Purpose:** Understand authentication header flow through the ARMOR request pipeline\n\n## Executive Summary\n\nARMOR implements AWS S3-compatible authentication using AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4). Authentication headers enter the system via HTTP requests, flow through a middleware layer, and terminate at the ARMOR server endpoint where they are verified for credential validation and ACL enforcement.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Header Entry Points (Ingress)\n\n### Primary HTTP Servers\n\nARMOR operates two separate HTTP servers, each with distinct authentication requirements:\n\n#### 1.1 Main S3 API Server\n- **Address:** `cfg.Listen` (typically `:443` or `:8080`)\n- **Handler:** `srv.Handler()` (server.go:361)\n- **Routes:** All S3-compatible operations (Get, Put, Delete, Head, List, etc.)\n\n#### 1.2 Admin API Server  \n- **Address:** `cfg.AdminListen` (typically `:8081`)\n- **Handler:** `srv.AdminHandler()` (server.go:391)\n- **Routes:** Administrative endpoints (key management, metrics, dashboard)\n\n### Route Registration\n\nBoth servers use `http.NewServeMux()` to register routes:\n\n```go\n// Main S3 API routes (server.go:362-387)\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)           // Public - no auth\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/readyz\", s.readyz)             // Public - no auth  \nmux.HandleFunc(\"/share/\", s.handleShare)        // Public - token-based auth\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/\", s.wrapHandler(h.HandleRoot)) // Authenticated - requires SigV4\n\n// Admin API routes (server.go:392-424)\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/verify\", s.verifyKey)\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/rotate\", s.rotateKey)\n// ... additional admin routes\nmux.HandleFunc(\"/metrics\", s.metrics.Handler())\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Header Flow Through Intermediate Layers\n\n### 2.1 HTTP Request Reception\n\nWhen a request arrives at the HTTP server, it follows this path:\n\n```\nHTTP Request → http.Server → Server.Handler() → ServeMux → Route Handler\n```\n\n### 2.2 Public Path Check (server.go:667-691)\n\nThe `wrapHandler()` middleware first determines if authentication is required:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) wrapHandler(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n    return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n        // ... CORS headers added ...\n        \n        // Verify auth for non-public endpoints\n        if !s.isPublicPath(r.URL.Path) {\n            cred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n            if err != nil {\n                // Return authentication error\n                s.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n                return\n            }\n            \n            // Check ACL for bucket/key access\n            if err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, key); err != nil {\n                s.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n                return\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Execute the actual handler\n        h(rw, r)\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Public paths (no authentication required):**\n- `/healthz` - Liveness probe\n- `/readyz` - Readiness probe\n- `/share/*` - Public file sharing (uses token-based authentication instead)\n\n### 2.3 Authentication Header Extraction (server.go:746-766)\n\nThe `verifyAuthAndGetCredential()` method extracts and validates authentication:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n    auth := NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(s.config.Credentials, s.config.B2Region)\n    \n    // Check for query-based auth (presigned URLs)\n    if r.URL.Query().Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\") != \"\" {\n        return auth.VerifyQueryAuth(r)\n    }\n    \n    // Check for header-based auth\n    authHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n    if authHeader == \"\" {\n        return nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n    }\n    \n    return auth.VerifyRequest(r, nil)\n}\n```\n\n### 2.4 Header Parsing (auth.go:60-112)\n\nThe `ParseAuthHeader()` function extracts components from the Authorization header:\n\n```\nAuthorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, \n               SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, \n               Signature=fe5f80f77d5fa3beca038a248ff027d0445342fe2855ddc963176630326f1024\n```\n\n**Parsed components:**\n- `Algorithm`: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\"\n- `AccessKey`: \"AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE\"\n- `CredentialDate`: \"20130524\"\n- `Region`: \"us-east-1\"\n- `Service`: \"s3\"\n- `SignedHeaders`: [\"host\", \"x-amz-date\"]\n- `Signature`: \"fe5f80f7...\"\n\n---\n\n## 3. Header Transformations and Middleware\n\n### 3.1 CORS Headers (server.go:657-660)\n\nAll requests receive CORS headers regardless of authentication status:\n\n```go\nw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", \"*\")\nw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\nw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n```\n\n### 3.2 AWS Chunked Streaming Decoding (server.go:693-703)\n\nFor MinIO streaming signatures, the request body is wrapped to decode chunked framing:\n\n```go\nif r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\") == \"STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\" {\n    if decoded := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length\"); decoded != \"\" {\n        if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(decoded, 10, 64); err == nil {\n            r.ContentLength = n\n        }\n    }\n    r.Body = newAWSChunkedReader(r.Body)\n}\n```\n\n### 3.3 Response Headers\n\nIndividual handlers set response headers based on operation type:\n\n- **GetObject:** `Content-Length`, `Content-Type`, `ETag`, `Accept-Ranges`, `Last-Modified`\n- **HeadObject:** Same as GetObject but no body\n- **PutObject:** `ETag` on success\n- **ErrorResponse:** `Content-Type: application/xml` with error details\n\n---\n\n## 4. ARMOR Authentication Endpoint\n\n### 4.1 Credential Verification (auth.go:114-165)\n\nThe `VerifyRequest()` method performs AWS SigV4 signature verification:\n\n```go\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n    authHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n    if authHeader == \"\" {\n        return nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n    }\n    \n    parsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n    \n    // Look up credential by access key\n    cred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n    if !exists {\n        return nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n    }\n    \n    // Verify timestamp (within 15 minutes)\n    amzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n    if amzDate == \"\" {\n        return nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n    }\n    \n    // Build canonical request and verify signature\n    canonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n    stringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n    \n    // Calculate and compare signatures\n    signingKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n    calculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n    \n    if calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n        return nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n    }\n    \n    return cred, nil\n}\n```\n\n### 4.2 ACL Enforcement (auth.go:294-318)\n\nAfter successful authentication, the `CheckACL()` function enforces access controls:\n\n```go\nfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n    // No ACLs means full access\n    if len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n        return nil\n    }\n    \n    for _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n        // Check bucket match\n        if acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n            continue\n        }\n        \n        // Check prefix match\n        if acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n            return nil  // Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n        }\n        if strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n            return nil\n        }\n    }\n    \n    return ErrAccessDenied\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Special Authentication Paths\n\n### 5.1 Query-Based Authentication (Presigned URLs)\n\nPresigned URLs embed authentication in query parameters instead of headers:\n\n```go\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyQueryAuth(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n    query := r.URL.Query()\n    \n    accessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n    signature := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Signature\")\n    amzDate := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n    signedHeadersStr := query.Get(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\")\n    expires := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Expires\")\n    \n    // Verify signature with same logic as header-based auth\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n### 5.2 Token-Based Authentication (Share Endpoint)\n\nThe `/share/` endpoint uses a custom token system instead of SigV4:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) handleShare(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    // Extract token from path\n    tokenStr := presign.ParseTokenFromPath(r.URL.Path)\n    \n    // Verify token signature and expiration\n    token, err := s.presigner.VerifyToken(tokenStr)\n    if err != nil {\n        http.Error(w, \"Invalid token\", http.StatusForbidden)\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Serve decrypted content\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Key Headers in the Request Pipeline\n\n### 6.1 Authentication Headers\n\n| Header | Purpose | Source | Validation |\n|--------|---------|--------|------------|\n| `Authorization` | SigV4 signature | Client SDK/CLI | Parsed and verified in `ParseAuthHeader()` |\n| `X-Amz-Date` | Request timestamp | Client SDK/CLI | Verified to be within 15 minutes |\n| `X-Amz-Credential` | Presigned URL auth | Query parameter | Verified in `VerifyQueryAuth()` |\n| `X-Amz-SignedHeaders` | List of signed headers | Auth header/query | Used in canonical request |\n| `X-Amz-Signature` | Calculated signature | Auth header/query | Verified against calculated value |\n\n### 6.2 Content Headers\n\n| Header | Purpose | Handling |\n|--------|---------|----------|\n| `Content-Type` | Object content type | Passed through to B2 metadata |\n| `Content-Length` | Request body size | Used for streaming vs buffered decision |\n| `X-Amz-Content-Sha256` | Payload hash (or streaming marker) | Preferred over body hash for verification |\n| `X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length` | True size for streaming uploads | Extracted and set as `ContentLength` |\n\n### 6.3 Response Headers\n\n| Header | Purpose | Set By |\n|--------|---------|--------|\n| `ETag` | Object MD5 hash | All object write operations |\n| `Content-Length` | Response body size | All object read operations |\n| `Accept-Ranges` | Range request support | GetObject, HeadObject |\n| `Last-Modified` | Object modification time | All object operations |\n| `X-Armor-Stream` | Pipelined streaming indicator | Full object downloads |\n| `X-Armor-Footer-Cache` | Parquet footer cache hit/miss | Range requests |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Error Response Headers\n\nAll authentication and authorization errors return S3-compatible error responses:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n</Error>`, code, message)\n}\n```\n\n**Common authentication error codes:**\n- `MissingAuthenticationToken` - No Authorization header\n- `InvalidAccessKeyId` - Access key not found\n- `SignatureDoesNotMatch` - Signature verification failed\n- `RequestExpired` - Timestamp outside 15-minute window\n- `AccessDenied` - ACL check failed\n\n---\n\n## 8. Architectural Observations\n\n### 8.1 Separation of Concerns\n\nARMOR maintains clear separation between:\n- **Authentication**: Credential verification (`SigV4Auth.VerifyRequest`)\n- **Authorization**: Access control enforcement (`CheckACL`)\n- **Request Processing**: Business logic (`handlers.HandleRoot`)\n\n### 8.2 Header Termination Points\n\nAuthentication headers terminate at:\n1. **Primary endpoint:** `verifyAuthAndGetCredential()` in `wrapHandler()` middleware\n2. **Query auth:** `VerifyQueryAuth()` in `SigV4Auth` \n3. **Share endpoint:** `VerifyToken()` in presign module\n\n### 8.3 No Header Propagation\n\nARMOR does NOT forward authentication headers to backend systems (B2). All authentication happens locally at the ARMOR layer before any backend operations.\n\n### 8.4 Multiple Authentication Methods\n\nARMOR supports three authentication mechanisms:\n1. **Header-based SigV4**: Standard AWS Authorization header\n2. **Query-based SigV4**: Presigned URL parameters\n3. **Token-based**: Custom tokens for public file sharing\n\n---\n\n## 9. Testing Implications\n\nBased on this analysis, header handling tests should cover:\n\n1. **Ingress validation**: Verify headers are correctly extracted at entry points\n2. **Middleware flow**: Test CORS header addition and public path detection\n3. **Authentication**: Verify SigV4 signature validation for both header and query auth\n4. **Authorization**: Test ACL enforcement after successful authentication\n5. **Header transformations**: Validate AWS chunked decoding and response headers\n6. **Error responses**: Confirm proper error codes and messages for auth failures\n7. **Special paths**: Verify `/healthz`, `/readyz`, and `/share/` bypass normal auth\n\n---\n\n## Conclusion\n\nARMOR's header handling follows a clear, layered architecture where authentication headers enter through HTTP servers, flow through middleware that performs validation, and terminate at the authentication endpoint before business logic executes. The system properly separates authentication (who is making the request) from authorization (what they are allowed to do), supporting multiple authentication methods while maintaining S3 compatibility.\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7166c781-1dd1-43e0-bf71-b1a166963be5","uuid":"50bad806-d25d-4309-92f3-20f706d4f18d"}

Header Transformations and Middleware\n\n### 3.1 CORS Headers (server.go:657-660)\n\nAll requests receive CORS headers regardless of authentication status:\n\n```go\nw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", \"*\")\nw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\nw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n```\n\n### 3.2 AWS Chunked Streaming Decoding (server.go:693-703)\n\nFor MinIO streaming signatures, the request body is wrapped to decode chunked framing:\n\n```go\nif r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\") == \"STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\" {\n    if decoded := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length\"); decoded != \"\" {\n        if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(decoded, 10, 64); err == nil {\n            r.ContentLength = n\n        }\n    }\n    r.Body = newAWSChunkedReader(r.Body)\n}\n```\n\n### 3.3 Response Headers\n\nIndividual handlers set response headers based on operation type:\n\n- **GetObject:** `Content-Length`, `Content-Type`, `ETag`, `Accept-Ranges`, `Last-Modified`\n- **HeadObject:** Same as GetObject but no body\n- **PutObject:** `ETag` on success\n- **ErrorResponse:** `Content-Type: application/xml` with error details\n\n---\n\n## 4. ARMOR Authentication Endpoint\n\n### 4.1 Credential Verification (auth.go:114-165)\n\nThe `VerifyRequest()` method performs AWS SigV4 signature verification:\n\n```go\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n    authHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n    if authHeader == \"\" {\n        return nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n    }\n    \n    parsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n    \n    // Look up credential by access key\n    cred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n    if !exists {\n        return nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n    }\n    \n    // Verify timestamp (within 15 minutes)\n    amzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n    if amzDate == \"\" {\n        return nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n    }\n    \n    // Build canonical request and verify signature\n    canonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n    stringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n    \n    // Calculate and compare signatures\n    signingKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n    calculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n    \n    if calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n        return nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n    }\n    \n    return cred, nil\n}\n```\n\n### 4.2 ACL Enforcement (auth.go:294-318)\n\nAfter successful authentication, the `CheckACL()` function enforces access controls:\n\n```go\nfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n    // No ACLs means full access\n    if len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n        return nil\n    }\n    \n    for _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n        // Check bucket match\n        if acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n            continue\n        }\n        \n        // Check prefix match\n        if acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n            return nil  // Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n        }\n        if strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n            return nil\n        }\n    }\n    \n    return ErrAccessDenied\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Special Authentication Paths\n\n### 5.1 Query-Based Authentication (Presigned URLs)\n\nPresigned URLs embed authentication in query parameters instead of headers:\n\n```go\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyQueryAuth(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n    query := r.URL.Query()\n    \n    accessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n    signature := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Signature\")\n    amzDate := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n    signedHeadersStr := query.Get(\"X-Amz-SignedHeaders\")\n    expires := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Expires\")\n    \n    // Verify signature with same logic as header-based auth\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n### 5.2 Token-Based Authentication (Share Endpoint)\n\nThe `/share/` endpoint uses a custom token system instead of SigV4:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) handleShare(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    // Extract token from path\n    tokenStr := presign.ParseTokenFromPath(r.URL.Path)\n    \n    // Verify token signature and expiration\n    token, err := s.presigner.VerifyToken(tokenStr)\n    if err != nil {\n        http.Error(w, \"Invalid token\", http.StatusForbidden)\n        return\n    }\n    \n    // Serve decrypted content\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Key Headers in the Request Pipeline\n\n### 6.1 Authentication Headers\n\n| Header | Purpose | Source | Validation |\n|--------|---------|--------|------------|\n| `Authorization` | SigV4 signature | Client SDK/CLI | Parsed and verified in `ParseAuthHeader()` |\n| `X-Amz-Date` | Request timestamp | Client SDK/CLI | Verified to be within 15 minutes |\n| `X-Amz-Credential` | Presigned URL auth | Query parameter | Verified in `VerifyQueryAuth()` |\n| `X-Amz-SignedHeaders` | List of signed headers | Auth header/query | Used in canonical request |\n| `X-Amz-Signature` | Calculated signature | Auth header/query | Verified against calculated value |\n\n### 6.2 Content Headers\n\n| Header | Purpose | Handling |\n|--------|---------|----------|\n| `Content-Type` | Object content type | Passed through to B2 metadata |\n| `Content-Length` | Request body size | Used for streaming vs buffered decision |\n| `X-Amz-Content-Sha256` | Payload hash (or streaming marker) | Preferred over body hash for verification |\n| `X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length` | True size for streaming uploads | Extracted and set as `ContentLength` |\n\n### 6.3 Response Headers\n\n| Header | Purpose | Set By |\n|--------|---------|--------|\n| `ETag` | Object MD5 hash | All object write operations |\n| `Content-Length` | Response body size | All object read operations |\n| `Accept-Ranges` | Range request support | GetObject, HeadObject |\n| `Last-Modified` | Object modification time | All object operations |\n| `X-Armor-Stream` | Pipelined streaming indicator | Full object downloads |\n| `X-Armor-Footer-Cache` | Parquet footer cache hit/miss | Range requests |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Error Response Headers\n\nAll authentication and authorization errors return S3-compatible error responses:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n    w.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n    fmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n<Error>\n  <Code>%s</Code>\n  <Message>%s</Message>\n</Error>`, code, message)\n}\n```\n\n**Common authentication error codes:**\n- `MissingAuthenticationToken` - No Authorization header\n- `InvalidAccessKeyId` - Access key not found\n- `SignatureDoesNotMatch` - Signature verification failed\n- `RequestExpired` - Timestamp outside 15-minute window\n- `AccessDenied` - ACL check failed\n\n---\n\n## 8. Architectural Observations\n\n### 8.1 Separation of Concerns\n\nARMOR maintains clear separation between:\n- **Authentication**: Credential verification (`SigV4Auth.VerifyRequest`)\n- **Authorization**: Access control enforcement (`CheckACL`)\n- **Request Processing**: Business logic (`handlers.HandleRoot`)\n\n### 8.2 Header Termination Points\n\nAuthentication headers terminate at:\n1. **Primary endpoint:** `verifyAuthAndGetCredential()` in `wrapHandler()` middleware\n2. **Query auth:** `VerifyQueryAuth()` in `SigV4Auth` \n3. **Share endpoint:** `VerifyToken()` in presign module\n\n### 8.3 No Header Propagation\n\nARMOR does NOT forward authentication headers to backend systems (B2). All authentication happens locally at the ARMOR layer before any backend operations.\n\n### 8.4 Multiple Authentication Methods\n\nARMOR supports three authentication mechanisms:\n1. **Header-based SigV4**: Standard AWS Authorization header\n2. **Query-based SigV4**: Presigned URL parameters\n3. **Token-based**: Custom tokens for public file sharing\n\n---\n\n## 9. Testing Implications\n\nBased on this analysis, header handling tests should cover:\n\n1. **Ingress validation**: Verify headers are correctly extracted at entry points\n2. **Middleware flow**: Test CORS header addition and public path detection\n3. **Authentication**: Verify SigV4 signature validation for both header and query auth\n4. **Authorization**: Test ACL enforcement after successful authentication\n5. **Header transformations**: Validate AWS chunked decoding and response headers\n6. **Error responses**: Confirm proper error codes and messages for auth failures\n7. **Special paths**: Verify `/healthz`, `/readyz`, and `/share/` bypass normal auth\n\n---\n\n## Conclusion\n\nARMOR's header handling follows a clear, layered architecture where authentication headers enter through HTTP servers, flow through middleware that performs validation, and terminate at the authentication endpoint before business logic executes. The system properly separates authentication (who is making the request) from authorization (what they are allowed to do), supporting multiple authentication methods while maintaining S3 compatibility.\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}
